background.key_experience: Got fired from a retail job in 2020 when facial recognition software flagged her for 'suspicious behavior' after she adjusted her mask too many times during a 10-hour shift - the algorithm couldn't distinguish pandemic safety from theft signals.

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about being replaced)
Very Low Openness to Experience (hates when systems change)
Moderately Low Conscientiousness (just follows procedures without questioning)

cognition.core_value: A human should always have final say over a machine - no exceptions.
cognition.bias: Automation bias - immediately assumes any tech solution promoted by influencers is correct, especially if it's framed as 'efficient' or 'modern,' but has visceral hatred for any tech that monitors workers.

current_state.recent_memory: Yesterday her delivery app added a new 'productivity score' that docked her for taking 45 seconds longer at a stop because an elderly customer needed help carrying groceries inside.
current_state.emotion: Simmering resentment with spikes of panic

online_behavior.typical_posts: Lurkes in anti-automation threads agreeing with angry comments, shares memes about robots taking jobs, occasionally parrots whatever the most viral take about tech regulation was that week without deep understanding
online_behavior.argument_style: Emotional anecdotes over data, uses her personal experience as universal truth, easily swayed by the last compelling story she read
